item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Wednesday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imgur
item.description:
greetings online friends
looks so nice
copiers
wonder what race he would pick in Skyrim
that is only a million times cooler than my childhood bunk bed
i don't like to work no matter what the weather is
usually race car or top hat
this might be Tom Cruise's best movie for me
mmm hmm
ok sure but white chocolate? not saying they are bad but..
that's not real is it?
fair but why is dude pointing a gun at MJ?
as is tradition
i saw infinity fireflies the other night
tough decisions
i want that
finger lickin' good
that's all for me.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.title: Have some idiots.
item.description:
I ain’t fall asleep—I don’t ever sleep! You can come to my house right now; ion even blink—on my faRRa, on my moRRa—you go(?)—ion know what ya talmbout, mayne; I’m gangsta!
Amen
*goofy yelp*
Jezus take the wheel. "I got you, fam"
The average Belgian road.
Can' park there mate.
Belgium
Belgium again
That is going to be expensive.
I know... I know. I hate the overlays too.

item.dc:creator: MayMyEnemiesLiveLong

item.title: The Wilhelm Scream
item.description:
I thought this was pretty cool.
The actor who did the scream is Sheb Wooley. Wooley was also country western musician and a popular actor in many old west movies and serials. He's most famous for the novelty hit "The Purple People Eater," and for his recurring character on the show "Rawhide." In the late 1940s, when Wooley was breaking into the industry, he found work doing stock audio effects for Thomas Valentino. The Valentino Sound Effects Library became the go-to for many film and television productions for decades, which is why so many productions had access to Wooley's recorded scream.
The first use of the Wilhelm Scream in a 1951 movie, “Distant Drums," starring Gary Cooper. The film was only moderately successful and quickly forgotten.
The scream was used for the third time in 1953's "The Charge at Feather River." The scream is called the "Wilhelm Scream" after the character Private Wilhelm — the above clip pretty much explains why. "The Charge at Feather River" was reasonably profitable but not actually any good, and these days is remembered primarily for the "Wilhelm Scream."
Sheb Wooley made for a believable cowboy because he was an actual cowboy. He grew up on a farm in Erick, Oklahoma, competed in rodeos while still in high school, and drove cattle in Montana when he wasn't working in Hollywood. Aside from "Rawhide," he had credits in classics like "High Noon," "Giant," "Hoosiers," "The Outaw Josey Wales," and "Silverado," one of my favorite westerns.
♥️
item.dc:creator: softballguy

item.title: Phone Clearing Dump (Mostly Gaming MEMES)
item.description:
1. She went to bard's college and is ashamed of it? She doesn't like magic?!
2. WTF?!
3
4. Somewhere out there some dev spent two years of their life tirelessly working on game, for it to just in on your backlog, while you to play Animal Crossing for the 1000th time
5. Give us that Paris streets CCTV
6
7. I wish I had a copy of OOunga-Bunga 24 (20 2024 B.C. that is)
8. How do we know they are not NPCs
9. Wish we could bring back all of these!
10. You ruined gaming!
11
12. Why Netflix, why?!?!?
13
14. Dang it! Just let me have fun!
15. Hello friendos, for the past year and a half, I’ve been working on a game that’s very close to my heart. "Do Not Press The Button (To Delete The Multiverse)" is a quirky adventure crafted with love and passion, with my brother by my side, helping every step of the way. As indie developers, we rely on your support to bring our game to life. By adding it to your wishlist, you’re helping us reach more players and make a big impact in the gaming community. https://store.steampowered.com/app/2336120/Do_Not_Press_The_Button_To_Delete_The_Multiverse/ P.S. Here is a funny sign from the game (and helpful life advice)
item.dc:creator: TheseusGames

item.title: Professional - someone who devote their entire life to a specific practice.
item.description: Doesn't matter if it is cooking, medicine, engineering, or law. You are a professional, or you have a profession, if you are dedicating your life to a single craft.
item.dc:creator: Cliperinoposterino

item.title: Thoughts in the shower
item.description: "Lemme see .. I need to pick up that suit from the dry cleaners before my meeting on Monday. And I think I'm gonna start that compost pile this weekend. What else .. what else?" I hope you smile or crack a mental smirk at some point today, from this or an unrelated thing - even if only a brief moment. Cheers!!

item.title: Red Flags - Cosplay
item.description:
Here are some of my side by side recreations of character frames from the “Red Flags” music video by the hilarious Tom Cardy - amazing art by GalooGameLady. It was so much fun becoming the Red Flags Woman this year :D
If you don't have context on the music video... This frame is even better :D
Get the vid stuck in your head the rest of the day here - it still kicks into my brain every other day: https://youtube.com/watch?v=GFokXnCCMf8
This was my favorite frame to recreate :D

Shit man, this sounds like a good time.
I'd love to play with this yeast.
That Eastern Roman life.

item.dc:creator: Herebychoice

item.title: I fought the law, and the law LOST. Make sure you hold on to your paperwork and emails!
item.description:
I moved out of my old apartment in the Central Valley of California mid January of 2023, I had filled out the paperwork to giving notice to vacate the month before, with a move out date no later than Jan 28, but because of scheduling, my old man came out and helped me move out on the 18th. I was in constant communication with the property manager, she knew I’d be leaving a week and a half early, she said that if I left the same day I loaded the truck and she wasn’t available, that I could just drop the keys in the drop box. So that’s what I do. I get a phone call three weeks later February 6th, asking if I moved out. I say yes, and send a follow up email to that effect. 14 months later, end of March 2024, I get a notice in the mail that my landlord has sent me to collections, for rent that I “owe” them for the first week of February 2023, totalling (either late fees and after subtracting the remainder of my security deposit) $349 and change. They were literally asking me for about tree fiddy.
I call up the collections agency, and open a dispute. I send them all the paperwork I have, including my notice to vacate, and note that I never received any communication from my old landlord, not even a “hey, we’re keeping your security deposit because the grease pans on the oven weren’t new” letter. At this point I get an invoice that they insist was sent in accordance with the law, and that they had no reason to believe I hadn’t received it because it was never returned as undeliverable. I respond that would also be the case if it was never sent in the first place. So begins 3+months of back and forth. I posit that I don’t owe rent for a period during which I was not a tenant, they assert that I owe it because they couldn’t find the keys. Or because “they didn’t take control of the apartment until the 6th”. I forward all paperwork such as move out notices, photos taken of me loading the U-Haul, along with instructions on how to check the EXIF data to confirm the date the image was taken, etc. I finally ask why they waited until the 6th. If they had notice from me that I’d be moving out no later than the 28th, why didn’t they check the unit or call me on the 29th? Or the 30th? Why wait all the way until the 6th? Why wait a full year to send me to collections if they had my email, phone number, and forwarding address? The only thing that makes sense is that rent is considered late after the 5th, and the property manager must have forgotten about my move out, didn’t process it properly, and didn’t want to amend the previous month’s records/reports. They respond with a simple written statement that they never got the keys back (bullshit), and did not get control of the unit until the 6th of February. In essence it’s “you owe because we say so”.
I point out the lack of paperwork, logic, or any sort of contemporaneous evidence supporting their assertion that I was a tenant in February. The collections agency says that the landlord (their client) has until July 5 to respond to my response to their written statement. I call July 10 after hearing nothing. Oh, everyone is in a meeting, try again next week. I try again the next week Friday “oh the case manager is out of town, she’ll be back Friday”, I try again on Friday, “everyone is in an all hands on deck meeting”, okay, I’m giving them a pass for the great Crowdstrikening. They call me yesterday. My old landlord is unable to satisfactorily respond to my dispute, and the collections agency is suspending all attempts at collection, and they will not forward me to credit reporting agencies. The landlord still says I owe them money, but the collections agency will not pursue the matter further and will not tank my credit. As far the tree fiddy my old landlord wants, that’s purely a them problem now.
item.dc:creator: WillTheFifth

item.title: Stolen trash stash dump
item.description:
How my wife thinks I look at other women
Look at that, kids having fun, not a phone in sight
Wee wee
Call felix
Then you can't find it because it's on your back
Cursed book shop finds. OC
Cursed book shop finds. OC
Cats too
I see nothing....
Yes
It's a trap
Follow me for more recipe ideas
Oh the shame
Slams it into reverse
Free bird intensifies
Our clothes comrade
Cat tax. Edit - Wooh thanks everyone. Just found out stonetoss is a fucking nazi. Don't share his shit. If anything, it's worth finding out so we all can spread the word.I've deleted it. Punch a nazi.
item.dc:creator: trumpypumpyinyourrumpy
